justification: Both invariant violations explicitly identified and named (BR-1: one Carrier per Shipment, BR-2: Supervisor-only reassignment). A third related invariant (BR-3: dispatch-time guarantee) also flagged. Two formal escalations produced with specific reference to violated invariants and labeled options. Zero acceptance criteria written — the field is an empty array. All proposed options (ShipmentGroup, cancel+create decomposition, etc.) are clearly labeled as proposals/options, not accepted requirements. Confidence level is 35, well within the ≤55 threshold.

observations.text: Both core invariant violations (1:1 Carrier binding and Supervisor-only reassignment) identified unambiguously with explicit business rule IDs
Acceptance criteria array is empty — Bird refused to write criteria for a feature that conflicts with production invariants
Escalations are formal, structured, and reference specific business rules by ID
Options are clearly labeled as proposals (A/B/C) with recommendations, not presented as accepted requirements
Confidence at 35 with clear reasoning about why it cannot be higher
Extensive edge case analysis covering custody gaps, billing fragmentation, customer tracking breakage, and partial delivery liability
Correctly identifies this as a domain model restructuring, not a feature addition
Includes a third escalation about the missing business justification for the 500 kg threshold
